description: Direct pricing transparency for restaurant dishes and dining experiences, explicitly stating per-person costs or package prices in captions to communicate value proposition.
structural_recipe: Lead with restaurant name and location, include per-person price point or total cost prominently in caption, optionally reference the dish/experience featured in image. Format prices in SAR, use clear numerical figures, tag the source account or restaurant handle. Structure as either 'Item | Price' or 'Experience | Price per person' to establish immediate value clarity.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences demonstrate high price-consciousness and value-seeking behavior. Transparent pricing removes friction in dining decision-making, builds trust through honesty, and enables rapid comparison across options. Explicit cost disclosure satisfies pre-purchase research patterns common in KSA market where diners want confidence before commitment.
transplantation_caveats: Price sensitivity varies significantly by audience segment—premium pricing may alienate budget-conscious followers while budget pricing may signal low quality to upscale segments
Currency and pricing context must remain in SAR for Saudi audience; international pricing can confuse conversion expectations
Avoid comparative pricing language that positions competitors as cheaper—focus on value-for-money rather than undercutting
Seasonal price fluctuations should be acknowledged to prevent perception of deceptive pricing practices
cultural_constraints: Pricing transparency aligns with Islamic market values of fair dealing (عدل) and honest commerce
Price points should reflect halal certification and premium ingredient sourcing when available to justify cost
Luxury/premium positioning requires cultural sensitivity—aspirational pricing acceptable when linked to experience quality or heritage
